title: calamintha nepeta var. canescens
intro: Calamintha nepeta var. canescens, also known as mountain mint, is a subspecies of the Calamintha nepeta plant, a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of Europe. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to stimulate digestion, reduce anxiety, and alleviate respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds. The health benefits of Calamintha nepeta var. canescens include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating wounds, reducing fever, and soothing sore throats. Calamintha nepeta var. canescens can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often used in herbal remedies for its calming and invigorating effects.
